---
title: "Section 11: Data Import"
author: "Matt Higham"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Section 11.1: `readr`

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
##cars_df <- read_csv(here("data/mtcarsex.csv"),
##                    skip = 2,  ## ignores the first two rows
##                    na = c("NA", " ", -999))

cars_df <- read_csv(here("data/mtcarsex.csv"),
         skip = 2,  ## ignores the first two rows
         na = c("NA", " ", -999),
         col_types = cols(
           mpg = col_double(),
           cyl = col_factor(),
           disp = col_double(),
           hp = col_double(),
           drat = col_double(),
           wt = col_double(),
           qsec = col_double(),
           vs = col_logical(),
           am = col_logical(),
           gear = col_double(),
           carb = col_double()
         )) |>
  slice(-c(1, 2))
cars_df
```

Exercise 1.

```{r}
fitness_df <- read_csv(here::here("data/higham_fitness_notclean.csv"),
                       col_types = cols(
                         Date = col_date(format = ""),
                         active_cals = col_double(),
                         flights = col_double(),
                         steps = col_double(),
                         distance = col_double(),
                         month = col_factor(),
                         weekday = col_factor(),
                         dayofyear = col_double(),
                         year = col_double(),
                         stepgoal = col_logical()
                       ))
fitness_df
```

## Section 11.2: Data Scraping

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(rvest)

## provide the URL and name it something (in this case, url).
url <- "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gun_violence_in_the_United_States_by_state"

## read_html() convert the html code from the URL into something R can read
tab <- read_html(url) |> 
  html_nodes("table") ## html_nodes can grab only the tables 

tab

tab[[1]] |> html_table() ## convert an html table into an r data frame
tab[[2]] |> html_table()
tab[[3]] |> html_table()
```

```{r}
url <- "https://saintsathletics.com/sports/baseball/stats/2025"

tab <- read_html(url) |> html_nodes("table")
tab
obj <- tab[[1]] |> html_table()
obj
obj2 <- tab[[2]] |> html_table()
obj2
```


```{r}
url_diversity <- "https://www.stlawu.edu/offices/institutional-research/student-diversity-2021"
tab_diversity <- read_html(url_diversity) |> html_nodes("table")

tab_diversity[[1]] |> html_table()
```

## Section 11.3

Skip.



## Class Exercises

Class Exercise 1

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
df <- read_delim(here("data/birthdays.txt"), delim = " - ",
                 skip = 4,
                 col_names = c("birthday", "name", "species", "time"),
                 na = c("?", "N/A", " "),
                 col_types = cols(
                   birthday = col_character(), ## deal with dates with lubridate
                   name = col_character(),
                   species = col_factor(),
                   time = col_character()
                 )) |>
  mutate(time = parse_number(time))
df
```

Class Exercise 4.

```{r}
url_tennis <- "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Current_tennis_rankings"
tab_tennis <- read_html(url_tennis) |> html_nodes("table")

## specifying what I want the new names to be
new_names <- c("number", "player", "points", "move")

tennis_df <- tab_tennis[[1]] |> html_table(header = FALSE) |>
  set_names(new_names) |> ## setting those new_names as the column names
  slice(-c(1:2))
tennis_clean <- tennis_df |> mutate(points = parse_number(points)) |>
  mutate(player = fct_reorder(player, points))
  

ggplot(data = tennis_clean, aes(x = points, y = player)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#CCFF00", colour = "black") +
  theme_minimal()
```
